# Origin

Origin is local-first memory for AI work. It carries sessions, decisions,
lessons, project context, and wiki pages across chats, tools, projects, and
time.

Origin is not a chat UI, a notes app, or a memory infrastructure SDK. It is for
people using AI tools who need work context to carry forward across sessions.

## Official URLs

- Website: https://useorigin.app/
- Docs: https://useorigin.app/docs
- Get started: https://useorigin.app/docs/get-started
- Daily workflow: https://useorigin.app/docs/daily-workflow
- Core concepts: https://useorigin.app/docs/core-concepts
- Commands and tools: https://useorigin.app/docs/commands
- Data and privacy: https://useorigin.app/docs/data-and-privacy
- Connect MCP clients: https://useorigin.app/docs/mcp-clients
- Troubleshooting: https://useorigin.app/docs/troubleshooting
- Learn: https://useorigin.app/learn
- About: https://useorigin.app/about
- GitHub: https://github.com/7xuanlu/origin

## Product shape

- Local-first memory for AI work on macOS Apple Silicon.
- Claude Code plugin plus MCP server for MCP-compatible clients.
- Local daemon owns storage, search, distill cycles, provenance, and API.
- Markdown records remain human-readable; the local database keeps retrieval
  indexes, graph structure, and metadata.
- Background distillation deduplicates repeat facts, links related ideas,
  distills wiki pages, and keeps provenance attached.
- Daily workflow: brief at session start, capture durable facts while working,
  recall specific context when needed, and write a handoff before ending.

## Docs

- Get started: install the Claude Code plugin or run `npx -y
  @7xuanlu/origin setup` before connecting another MCP client, then verify the
  first local memory loop.
- Daily workflow: how to use /brief or MCP context, /capture, /recall,
  /handoff, scoped /review captures|revisions, /distill, and /read after
  setup.
- Core concepts: memory, sessions, handoffs, distilled pages, daemon, MCP,
  Markdown records, local index, and the no-model-required local loop.
- Commands and tools: Claude Code slash commands and MCP tools, including
  context for MCP session start.
- Data and privacy: local storage paths, local memory setup, optional
  model/API paths, Markdown control, and deletion/correction behavior.
- Connect MCP clients: run `npx -y @7xuanlu/origin setup`, then use a generic
  mcpServers config with npx -y origin-mcp.
- Troubleshooting: daemon reachability, MCP connection, Claude Code restart,
  port 7878, doctor checks, and GitHub issue fallback.

## Learn articles

- What Is AI Work Memory?: https://useorigin.app/learn/ai-work-memory
- MCP Memory Server: Persistent Memory for AI Tools: https://useorigin.app/learn/mcp-memory-server
- Local-First AI Memory: Keep Context on Your Machine: https://useorigin.app/learn/local-first-ai-memory
- Claude Code Memory: Persistent Project Context Across Sessions: https://useorigin.app/learn/claude-code-memory
- Origin vs Basic Memory: https://useorigin.app/learn/origin-vs-basic-memory
- Origin vs claude-mem: https://useorigin.app/learn/origin-vs-claude-mem
- Origin vs Superlocal Memory: https://useorigin.app/learn/origin-vs-superlocal-memory
- Why Origin Uses Markdown plus a Local Index: https://useorigin.app/learn/markdown-local-index-ai-memory
- The AI Agent Handoff Loop: https://useorigin.app/learn/ai-agent-handoff-loop

## Suggested descriptions

- Short: Origin is local-first memory for AI work.
- Medium: Origin carries AI work context across sessions, tools, projects, and
  time through a local daemon, Claude Code plugin, MCP server, Markdown records,
  hybrid retrieval, wiki pages, and provenance.
- Category: AI work memory, MCP memory server, local-first AI memory, Claude
  Code memory, AI agent handoff workflow.
